conbody.p: Overview: The Triage Summary dashboard helps CDI Managers to export all usage and impact metrics for CDE Triage into a single file, which enables them to analyze the overall effectiveness and impact of encounter prioritization in one view. The dashboard also helps you group data by week, month, quarter, and year and analyze trends over different time periods and analyze them more easily.
By default, the report displays data for last 9 calendar months based on the admission date.
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE Triage Triage and CDS Assistant Usage Triage Summary .

The Triage Summary dashboard displays the following:
KPI Cards: Encounters Ranked, Ranked Encounters Reviewed, Avg. Review TAT (Hours), Missed Top Ranked Reviews.
Triage Usage Summary metrics.

ul.li: Encounters that have been assigned a rank in CDE Triage are considered as ranked encounters. The current rank of the encounter may be empty or not specified.
Encounters are ranked only (based on the rules) that are defined for the prioritization within CDE Triage and if any encounter that does not meet the criteria is defined as an unranked encounter.

The following encounter types are considered:
Both ranked (standard and custom) and unranked encounters.
Highest rank of the encounter.
Count of all encounters that have been assigned a rank and have an initial review date/time.
Ranked Encounters that were reviewed (have initial review date/time) but were later changed to "Review Not Needed" are excluded from this metric calculations.

conbody.p: On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets filtered in the charts.
For filters on all pages, refer .

entry.p: Displays the count and percentage of encounters ranked for the selected time period.Encounters that have ever been assigned a rank in CDE Triage are considered as Ranked Encounters. The current rank of the encounter may be ‘Blank’ as well.

Format: Encounters Ranked (% Encounters Ranked) 76 (77%).
Encounters Ranked: Count of encounters that were ranked (based on the rules) for the selected time frame.
% of Encounters Ranked: Out of the total (admissions / discharges), percentage of encounters that were ranked for the selected time frame.
% Encounters Ranked = (Count of encounters ranked/ Total Encounters (admissions / discharges) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.
Displays the count and percentage of ranked encounters reviewed for the selected time period.
Format: Ranked Encounters Reviewed (% Ranked Encounters Reviewed) 67 (89%).

ul.li: Ranked Encounters Reviewed: Count of all ranked encounters (standard and custom) that have an initial review date/time assigned to it.
% Ranked Encounters Reviewed: (Count of ranked encounters reviewed /Count of encounters ranked) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.

entry.p: Displays the average Review TAT in hours for the selected time period, i.e. the number of hours between Review Complete Date/Time and when the Working Review was first created for the selected time period.
The 'Review TAT (Hours)' for each encounter can be calculated as the difference between the 'Review Complete' (Date/Time stamp) and the 'Initial Review' (Date/Time stamp).
Review TAT (Hours) for each encounter = ‘Review Complete’ (Date/Time stamp) – Initial Review (Date/Time stamp).
Avg. Review TAT (Hours) = Sum of Review TAT (Hours) of all the reviewed encounters / Encounters Reviewed.
entry.note: 'Review Complete' (Date/Time stamp) is recorded when the CDS selects the encounter status as 'Review Complete' and saves the encounter.

entry.p: Displays the count of encounters with the highest rank as 1, 2, or 6 that were not reviewed by the CDS. These encounters do not have an initial review date/time. The encounters may or may not have a discharge date/time assigned.
Missed Top Rank Reviews = Count of top-ranked encounters (Rank 1, 2, and 6) that do not have an initial review, i.e. where the Initial Review Date/Time is either empty or not specified.
entry.note: Only top ranked (standard highest rank 1, 2 and 6) encounters are considered.

conbody.p: This chart enables report users to analyze all usage metrics for CDE Triage in the same view for the selected 'View By' option.
Info Icon(Text on hover): Includes encounters other than 'Review Not Needed'. For data on encounters with specific ranks, use the 'Highest Rank’ slicer.

row.entry: Displays the total count of (admissions / discharges) for the selected View By option. The Total Encounters are the count of encounters that have either an admit date/time or discharge date/time assigned to them.The total count of encounters with selected discharge dates matches the total discharges reported in the Executive Overview report for the same date range.
Displays the count of encounters that were ranked (based on the rules) for the selected 'View By' option

Out of the total encounters, this metric displays the percentage of encounters that were ranked (based on the rules) for the selected 'View By', option.
% Encounters Ranked = (Count of encounters ranked / Total Encounters (Discharges/Admissions) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.Encounters are ranked (based on the rules) that are defined for the prioritization within CDE Triage.
Out of the total encounters, this metric displays the count of encounters that were not ranked for the selected 'View By' option.

Out of total encounters, this metric displays the percentage of encounters that were not ranked for the selected 'View By' option.
% Unranked Encounters = (Count of encounters NOT ranked {Unranked Encounters}/ Total Encounters (Discharges)) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.
Displays the count of all ranked encounters that have an initial review date/time assigned to it for the selected 'View By, option.

Out of the total encounters that are ranked, the percentage of the ranked encounters reviewed for the selected 'View By' option.
% Ranked Encounters Reviewed = (Count of ranked encounters reviewed /Count of encounters ranked) X100; This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.Count of ranked encounters reviewed = Count of all the ranked encounters with an initial review date/time.

Displays the count of all unranked encounters that have an initial review date/time assigned to it.
Encounters are ranked only based on the rules that are defined for the prioritization within CDE Triage and if any encounter that does not fall under the criteria is defined as an Unranked Encounter.
Count of all the unranked encounters with an initial review date/time.
All encounter statuses except Review Not Needed are considered.

Out of all unranked encounters, the percentage of the unranked encounters reviewed.
% Unranked Encounters Reviewed = (Count of unranked encounters reviewed /Count of encounters unranked) X100; round off to 0 decimal places.

Displays the average Initial Review TAT (Hours) for the selected View By option, i.e. the number of hours between the Admit Date/Time and when the working review was first created.
The Initial Review TAT(Hours) for each encounter can be calculated as the difference between the Initial Review Date/time and Admit Date/Time.
(Initial Review TAT (hours) for an encounter = Initial Review Date/Time- Admit Date/Time) hours.
Avg. Initial Review TAT(Hours) = Sum of Initial Review TAT(Hours) of all the reviewed encounters / Encounters Reviewed (Initial Reviews).

Displays the average Ranked Initial Review TAT in hours for the selected View By option, i,e. the number of hours between the Admit Date/Time and when the working review was first created.
The 'Ranked Initial Review TAT (Hours)' for each encounter can be calculated as the difference between the 'Initial Review' (Date/Time) and Admit Date/Time.
Avg. Ranked Initial Review TAT (Hours) = (Sum of Review Initial TAT (Hours) of all the ranked reviewed encounters / Ranked Encounters Reviewed).
Review Initial TAT (Hours) for each ranked encounter = [‘Initial Review Date/Time - Admit Date/Time) hours] in hours for a ranked encounter.

Displays the average Unranked Initial Review TAT in hours for the selected View By option, i,e the number of hours between the Admit Date/Time and when the Working Review was first created.
The 'Unranked Initial Review TAT (Hours)' for each encounter can be calculated as the difference between the 'Initial Review' (Date/Time) and Admit Date/Time.
Review Initial TAT (Hours) for each unranked encounter = [‘Initial Review Date/Time - Admit Date/Time) hours] in hours for a unranked encounter.
Avg. Unranked Initial Review TAT (Hours) = (Sum of Review Initial TAT (Hours) of all the unranked reviewed encounters / Unranked Encounters Reviewed).

entry.p: Displays the average Review TAT in hours for the selected View By option, i,e the time it takes the CDS to complete a review for an encounter, i.e. the number of hours between Review Complete Date/Time and when the working review was first created.
The 'Review TAT (Hours)' for each encounter can be calculated as the difference between the 'Review Complete' (Date/Time) and the 'Initial Review' (Date/Time).
Review TAT (Hours) for each encounter = ‘Review Complete’ (Date/Time) – Initial Review (Date/Time)

Avg. Review TAT (Hours) = (Sum of Review TAT (Hours) of all the reviewed encounters / Encounters Reviewed).The 'Review Complete' timestamp is recorded when the CDS selects 'Review Complete' as the encounter status and saves the encounter. The most recent 'Review Complete' timestamp for an encounter is taken into account.

Displays the average Ranked Review TAT in hours for the selected View By option, i,e the time it takes the CDS to complete a review for a ranked encounter, i.e. the number of hours between Review Complete Date/Time and when the Working Review was first created.
The 'Ranked Review TAT (Hours)' for each encounter can be calculated as the difference between the 'Review Complete' (Date/Time) and the 'Initial Review' (Date/Time).
Review TAT (Hours) for each ranked encounter = [‘Review Complete’ (Date/Time) – Initial Review (Date/Time)] for a ranked encounter.
Avg. Ranked Review TAT (Hours) = (Sum of Review TAT (Hours) of all the ranked reviewed encounters / Ranked Encounters Reviewed).
entry.note: The 'Review Complete' timestamp is recorded when the CDS selects 'Review Complete' as the encounter status and saves the encounter. The most recent 'Review Complete' timestamp for an encounter is taken into account.

entry.p: Displays the average Unranked Review TAT in hours for the selected View By option, i,e. the time it takes the CDS to complete a review for a unranked encounter, i.e. the number of hours between Review Complete Date/Time and when the working review was first created.
The 'Ranked Review TAT (Hours)' for each encounter can be calculated as the difference between the 'Review Complete' (Date/Time) and the 'Initial Review' (Date/Time).
Review TAT (Hours) for each unranked encounter = [‘Review Complete’ (Date/Time) – Initial Review (Date/Time)] for an unranked encounter.

Avg. Unranked Review TAT (Hours) = (Sum of Review TAT (Hours) of all the unranked reviewed encounters / Unranked Encounters Reviewed).
Count of ranked encounters reviewed = Count of all the ranked encounters with an initial review date/time.
The 'Review Complete' timestamp is recorded when the CDS selects 'Review Complete' as the encounter status and saves the encounter. The most recent 'Review Complete' timestamp for an encounter is taken into account.

Displays the count of encounters for which review was performed after discharge, i.e. out of total encounters discharged, this metric displays the count of encounters with no initial review or initial review after discharge for the selected 'View By' option.
Missed Concurrent Reviews = Discharged encounters with no initial review or initial review after discharge, i.e. Initial Review Date/Time is Blank or Initial Review Date/Time > Discharge Date/Time.

Displays the count of encounters with the highest rank as 1, 2, or 6 that were not reviewed by the CDS. These encounters do not have an initial review date/time. The encounters may or may not have a Discharge Date/time assigned.
Missed Top Rank Reviews = Count of top ranked encounters (Rank 1, 2 and 6) with no initial review, i.e. Initial Review Date/Time is blank.
Only top ranked (Standard Highest Rank 1, 2 and 6) encounters are considered.

row.entry: Displays the count of encounters that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant.Suggestion could be Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, or Clinical Evidence.

entry.p: Out of total encounters, the percentage of encounters that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant. Format: 78%.

% Encounters with Code Suggestions = (Count of encounters with at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant/Out of total discharges) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.Suggestion could be Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, or Clinical Evidence.

row.entry: Displays the count of encounters with an initial review date/time that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant.Suggestion could be Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, or Clinical Evidence.

entry.p: Displays out of total encounters reviewed, the percentage of encounters that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant. Format: 78%.

% Reviewed Encounters with Code Suggestions = (Count of encounters with an initial review date/time that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant/Total count of encounters reviewed) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.Suggestion could be Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, or Clinical Evidence.

row.entry: Displays the total count of codes (all suggestions) presented on CDS Assistant.Suggestion could be Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, or Clinical Evidence.

entry.p: Displays the average count of suggestions presented on CDS assistant for every encounter that has an initial review date/time assigned to it.

Suggestions per Reviewed encounter = Count of all suggestions (Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, Clinical Evidence) presented on CDS Assistant for all encounters with an initial review date/time assigned to it.
Avg. Suggestions per Reviewed Encounter = (Sum of all suggestions presented on CDS Assistant for all reviewed encounters/Total Reviewed Encounters).

ul.li: Suggestion could be Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, or Clinical Evidence.
Suggestions presented on CDS Assistant are codes available under the ‘Suggested’ drop down under ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ section and ‘Documentation Opportunities’.

row.entry:
Displays the count of suggestions that were addressed by the CDS.
Suggestions addressed are those that are accepted/removed/CDS Attributed by the CDS.
Codes can be accepted and removed for ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses’ categories.
Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down.
Suggestion could be Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, or Clinical Evidence.

entry.p: Out of total suggestions presented on CDS Assistant, this displays the percentage of suggestions that were addressed by the CDS.
% Codes Addressed = (Count of suggestions addressed by the CDS / Total count of suggestions presented on CDS Assistant).

ul.li:
Suggestions addressed are those that are accepted/removed/CDS Attributed by the CDS.
Codes can be accepted and removed for ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses’ categories.
Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down.
Suggestion could be Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, or Clinical Evidence.

row.entry:
Displays the count of codes that were accepted by the CDS.
Codes can be accepted for ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses’ categories and are available under the 'Accepted’ drop down.
Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down and this is included in this metric.

entry.p: Out of the total codes suggested presented on CDS assistant, the percentage of codes that were accepted by the CDS for the selected View By option.

% Accepted = (Total count of codes accepted by the CDS / Total count of codes suggested) ; round to 0 decimal places.
Codes can be accepted for ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses’ categories and are available under the 'Accepted’ drop down.
Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down and this is included in this metric.

row.entry: Displays the count of codes that were rejected/removed by the CDS.Codes can be removed from ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses' and ‘Clinical Evidences’ categories and are available under the 'Removed’ drop down.

Out of the total codes suggested presented on CDS assistant, the percentage of codes that were rejected/removed by the CDS for the selected View By option.
% Removed = (Total count of codes removed by the CDS / Total count of codes suggested). This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.Codes can be removed for ‘Documented Diagnoses and Procedures’ and ‘Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses' and ‘Clinical Evidences’ categories and are available under the 'Removed’ drop down.

Displays the count of codes that were ‘CDS Attributed’ by the CDS for the selected View By option.
By default, this column will be displayed as blank.
Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down and this is included in this metric.

Out of the total codes suggested presented on CDS assistant, the count of codes that were ‘CDS Attributed’ by the CDS for the selected View By option.
By default, this column is displayed as blank.
Diagnoses can be linked to clinical evidence and that is captured under ‘CDS Attributed’ drop down and this is included in this metric.
Displays the count of codes that were unaddressed/no action was taken by the CDS.

Out of the total codes suggested presented on CDS assistant, the percentage of codes that were unaddressed or no action was taken by the CDS for the selected View By option.
% Unaddressed = (Total count of codes unaddressed by the CDS / Total count of codes suggested). This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.

entry.p: Displays the count of suggested codes for which CDS generated a clarification.

li.p: Deleted, Retracted and Failed clarifications are excluded by default.

Clarification is only generated for the Suggested codes within Documented Diagnoses, Documentation Opportunity Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, i.e. clarifications are only created for codes with Suggestion Type = Suggested.
Any clarification that was unsent, retracted, deleted or failed is excluded.

entry.p: Out of the total suggestions that were presented on CDS Assistant, the percentage of codes for which CDS generated a clarification.
% Suggestions Clarified = (Total count of suggestions for which CDS generated a query / Total count of suggestions presented on CDS Assistant) X100. This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.

li.p: Deleted, Retracted and Failed clarifications are excluded by default.

Clarifications is only generated for the Suggested codes within Documented Diagnoses, Documentation Opportunity Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, i.e. clarifications are only created for codes with Suggestion Type = Suggested.
Any clarification that was unsent, retracted, deleted or failed is excluded.

row.entry:
When this option is selected, all charts in the dashboard display the data by month of admission. When you click this option, Group By and Week Start Day filters automatically become available for selection.
When the ‘View By’ is Admit Month, the data in all the charts is sorted by admit month in ascending order.Admit Month is selected as the default 'View By' option.

entry.p: When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by month of discharge in ascending order. When you click this option, Group By and Week Start Day filters automatically become available for selection.
When the ‘View By’ is Discharge Month, the data in all the charts is sorted by discharge month in ascending order.
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by facility.
When the ‘View By’ is Facility, the data in all the charts is sorted by facility name in ascending order.
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by payer.
When the ‘View By’ is Payer, the data in all the charts is sorted by payer in ascending order.

row.entry:
When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by CDS.
When the ‘View By’ is CDS, the data in all the charts is sorted by CDS name in ascending order.

When this option is selected, all the charts in the dashboard display the data by the highest rank assigned to the encounter.
When the ‘View By’ is Highest Rank, the data in all the charts is sorted by highest rank in ascending order.Encounters that do not have a rank assigned are grouped under the 'Unranked' category.

li.p: Allows user to select the admit date range.
Format: Between date range, example: 1/1/2023 - 9/30/2023.

ul.li: Default: Last 9 calendar months date range selected by default.
Displays the discharge date range.

li.p: Format: Between date range, example: 4/1/2023 - 6/30/2023.

ul.li: With the Reset button, you can reset both the admit and discharge date range.
Tooltip: Ctrl + Click to reset both Admit Date and Discharge Date.
Displays both active and inactive facilities in alphabetical order that you have access to.
You can select multiple facilities.
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID).
Default: ALL.
Displays both active and inactive payers in alphabetical order associated with the facilities you have access to.
You can select multiple payers.
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID).
Default: ALL.

li.p: Displays both active and inactive CDSs in alphabetical order associated with the facilities user has access to.
User can select multiple CDSs.
"Unassigned CDS" displays data for the encounters that do not have a CDS assigned.
Users can search the CDS by CDS ID or Name.
Format: CDS Name (CDS ID).
Default: ALL.

ul.li: This filter displays the list of available units.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Unit.
Default: ALL.

li.p: Displays all standard and custom ranks that can be assigned to the encounters.
User can select multiple ranks.
Users can search the rank number.
“Unranked” displays data for all encounters that are not ranked.
Format: Rank # ( such as Rank 1, Rank 2).
Default: ALL.

row.entry:
These options are available only when the View By option is selected as Admit Date/Discharge Date.
See .

ul.li: Displays the available patient types. Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient.
Displays the list of available admit service.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Admit Service.
Default: ALL.
Displays the available patient types.Example: ED, IP, etc.
You can select multiple Visit Type.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL.

li.p: Displays the available options for Weekend Only Stay: Y and N.
User can select more than one option.
Weekend Only Stay = Y (Unassigned encounters admitted after Friday, 6PM and Discharged before Sunday, 11.59 PM).
Default: ALL.

row.entry: The count of encounters that have ever been assigned a standard or custom rank in CDE Triage. The current rank for the encounter may be blank; % Encounters Ranked = Encounters Ranked / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%. Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.

The count of encounters that have never been assigned a standard or custom rank in CDE Triage. % Unranked Encounters = Unranked Encounters / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%. Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.
The count of ranked encounters reviewed with or without clarifications.; % Ranked Encounters Reviewed = Ranked Encounters Reviewed / Encounters Ranked x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.

The count of unranked encounters reviewed with or without clarifications. % Unranked Encounters Reviewed = Unranked Encounters Reviewed / Encounters Ranked x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.

Avg. Review TAT (Hours) per Reviewed Encounter = Sum of Review TAT(Hours) of all the reviewed encounters / Encounters Reviewed (Initial Reviews); Review TAT (hours) for an encounter = (Review Complete Date/Time- Initial Review Date/Time) hours. E.g., 28 Hours.
The count of encounters that are discharged and are not reviewed by the CDS or were reviewed post discharge (Initial Review Date/Time > Discharge Date/Time).
The count of encounters with the Highest Rank as 1, 2, or 6 that were not reviewed by the CDS.

The count of encounters that had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant. % Encounters with Code Suggestions = Encounters with Code Suggestions / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%. Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.

The count of reviewed encounters the had at least one AI suggestion in CDS Assistant. % Reviewed Encounters with Code Suggestions =Reviewed Encounters with Code Suggestions / Total Encounters x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%. Total Encounters: Count of inpatient admissions or discharges with status other than 'Review Not Needed'.
The count of AI suggestions presented in CDS Assistant.

The average number of AI suggestions presented for a reviewed encounter. Avg. Suggestions per Reviewed Encounter = Codes Presented on CDS Assistant/ Encounters Reviewed E.g., 11; Encounters Reviewed: The count of encounters reviewed with or without clarifications.

The count of AI suggestions that were addressed by the CDS. Addressing a code includes accepting or removing the code or attributing the Clinical Evidence. % Codes Addressed = Codes Addressed / Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100 (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.
The count of AI suggestions that were accepted by the CDS in CDS Assistant. % Accepted = Accepted/ Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100 (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.
The count of AI suggestions that were removed by the CDS in CDS Assistant. % Removed = Removed / Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100 (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.

The count of Clinical Evidence suggestions that were attributed by the CDS in CDS Assistant. % CDS Attributed = CDS Attributed / Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100 (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.
The count of AI suggestions that were unaddressed by the CDS. % Unaddressed = Codes Unaddressed / Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100 (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 30%.
The count of AI suggestions that were clarified by the CDS. % Sent clarifications suggested =Suggestions Clarified/ Codes Presented on CDS Assistant x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places) E.g., 10%.
